rationale: Both papers are clearly about customer churn prediction/behavior (the citing study deploys a churn prediction tool in B2B sales; the cited study models repeat churn timing/reasons), so the citation could be conceptually relevant. However, the evidence provided is only a single DOI match with no in-text citation context or reference text, and the cited paper’s specific method/context (mixture cure-competing risks in telecom win-back) is not obviously central to the citing paper’s XGBoost field experiment.
